quotes: The keyboard shortcuts do actually work! Just not well.
Did you know that Steller's Jays and Blue Jays can hybridize?
Something done poorly on purpose is still bad.
If you do small things, even though big things exist: you're on your way to crushing it! -Tom Cardy
Don't go gentle into that good night. Rage, rage against the dying of the light.
I originally made this page with html tables.
Now where could my pipe be?
Have you looked at a bird today?
I joined the war on frames on the side of AJAX.
I looked up how to center an element with CSS at least 8 times in the making of this site.
They call me the gambler because I wanna die on a train.
Blue Jays are corvids, like Crows and Ravens, and similarly can learn to mimic sounds.
Steller's Jays might be getting renamed soon.
"Probably needed a little more time to bake in the egg, I think."
Purveyor of tumblr boyfailure TTRPG characters, apparently?
The rest is yet to come.
Is my tail too big? Nah, of course not.
I wish that they'd sack me, and leave me to sleep.
He's got his fix for cabin fever now!
Made with love, many peanuts, and a little bit of spite.
I add new quotes every once in a while. Unless I forget to do that.
exitText: Really really sure?
Okay then, close the tab or something, jeez. Javascript nor HTML can actually close normally opened windows.
...what?
I told you what to do. Go do it. Hit cancel. Or just close the tab.
You know, this button does actually close the window in some situations. Just not this one.
Can I help you? Hello? I'm a javascript confirmation box imbued with the spirit of Mark.
So... hello? Nice to meet you? I don't know who you are, but I imagine you probably know who I am since you're on my page.
It's... May 2026 here. What's the future like?
You can't really tell me anything. Past Mark didn't think to include an input box.
Okay. How about this. In... three notices, I'll swap the buttons. 'Okay' will close it, and 'Cancel' or whatever will keep me talking.
Got it? Three...
Two...
One... swap!
...ahah. Did I get you once at least? In case you didn't know, it's pretty limited what I can do here.
Like, okay. Mark absolutely could have made this a whole thing with branching paths and stuff. I could be like the voice of the hero and we could go slay a princess or something together.
Stanley came to a set of two buttons. He pressed the 'cancel' button and went on his way, living the rest of his life normally without listening to the silly words.
Haven't you been tempted to hit the checkbox that prevents me from making any more popups? It's, like, right there. Unless it isn't. I dunno what browser you're using.
...if you've gotten this far, maybe say hey? To, like, the real Mark. Or in the guestbook. I dunno.
I'm currently the real Mark, but by the time you're reading this, like I said, I'm just a linear path of text.
...I'm running out of, ah, things to say. Appreciate you sticking around to see what I had to say. Even if I tricked you and made you go through everything a second time. Hehe.
Like, I'd tell you some bird facts or something, but those are kinda more the domain of the quote box down below.
I've always had a hard time telling people goodbye. I usually wait for them to say it first, y'know?
Alright. I suppose this bit is running a bit long.
But for, ah... posterity, not counting the time I may or may not have made you restart...
